design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → Face tournament bracket of opponents → Defeat at least 2/3 rounds per match → Progress through increasingly difficult opponents → Defeat final boss Zeus
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time 1v1 combat with frame-data execution precision; adaptation to opponent patterns within time pressure (round timer); risk of losing match progress on defeat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial arts combat mastery across diverse fighting styles and character archetypes; tournament glory against historical and mythological fighters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes forward/backward dashing ('Jet' speed mechanic) as core mobility innovation; implements attack clashing/rebound system where simultaneous attacks create interactive moments; strict 2-out-of-3 round match structure with no continue within match; character roster drawn from historical/mythological figures rather than original creations, positioning fighting as cross-temporal competition

difficulty.lives_system: Health bar per round; matches require 2-of-3 round victories; no mid-match continue
difficulty.continues_system: Likely arcade-standard continue on match loss; Game Boy version adapts to handheld conventions
difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament mode checkpoint between opponent groups; player restarts tournament from beginning on final boss loss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides implicit difficulty scaling (different characters have different move sets and matchup viability); 'The FORGING OF WARRIORS' mode allows custom opponent selection for skill calibration

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament bracket structure: Four groups (3 opponents each, defeat 2+ to advance) → Captain Kidd (2+ rounds) → Hanzou (2+ rounds) → Jack & Ryofu → Zeus final boss. New characters and stages unlock via 'The FORGING OF WARRIORS' mode
replayability_hooks: 14 playable characters with distinct move sets and playstyles encourage character experimentation
Tournament mode presents different opponent matchups based on character selection
'The FORGING OF WARRIORS' arcade-style mode allows infinite custom rematches with any opponent
Multiplayer versus mode supports competitive replay between two players
Character-specific stages in 'The FORGING OF WARRIORS' mode

world_building_techniques: Opening plot exposition: Dr. Brown tournament succeeded, fighters returned to time periods, one year later Mr. Z sponsors new 'World Heroes Battle Fest' tournament over five days
Character roster selection communicates world-building: historical figures (Jeanne D'Arc, Rasputin, Captain Kidd) and mythological entities (Zeus) positioned as time-displaced competitors
Tournament framework narrative: millions watching, Mr. Z's hidden ambition for world domination, Mr. Z employs loyal servants (Jack, Ryofu) as obstacles
Boss progression hierarchy signals narrative stakes: defeat minions Jack & Ryofu before Mr. Z's ultimate threat Zeus

boss_design.philosophy: Boss battles positioned as narrative climax within tournament structure; final boss Zeus represents supreme mythological threat; penultimate opponents (Jack, Ryofu as Mr. Z's servants) serve as sub-boss gatekeepers
notable_bosses: Captain Kidd (tournament gate boss; requires 2-of-3 round victory)
Hanzou Hattori (tournament gate boss; requires 2-of-3 round victory)
Jack (Mr. Z's servant; final gauntlet opponent)
Ryofu (Mr. Z's servant; final gauntlet opponent)
Zeus (ultimate final boss; represents Mr. Z's power and world domination threat)

faction_indicators: Mr. Z's faction: Jack, Ryofu (loyal servants working toward world domination)
World Heroes fighters: 14 competing individuals across historical/mythological backgrounds
Dr. Brown's former tournament sponsors (implied prior authority, now displaced)
danger_signals: Mr. Z's 'callous ambition for world domination' explicitly stated as antagonistic force
World domination agenda threatened to 'eliminate anyone who dares to get in his ruthless way'
Hidden sponsorship deception (fighters unaware Mr. Z true sponsor until tournament begins)
Concentrated power in two servants (Jack, Ryofu) managing tournament logistics and enforcement
environmental_storytelling: Tournament stages from 'World Heroes 2' retain narrative context; new character stages introduce new geographical/cultural settings
Multi-day, multi-location tournament ('over the next five days in different parts of the world') suggests global scope and Mr. Z's international influence
Character stage selection in multiplayer communicates that fighters retain territorial/cultural identity despite time displacement
